summary: Matched the 136-byte ftCommon slice `ftCo_0C60` by moving two functions, `ftCo_800C60C8` and `ftCo_800C6110`, out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s` into a new C translation unit and header. The new code preserves the hammer-fall transition logic: `ftCo_800C60C8` checks `ftCo_80099F1C`, conditionally calls `ftCo_800C6110`, and returns a boolean; `ftCo_800C6110` gets the fighter, changes motion state to `ftCo_MS_HammerFall` with `Ft_MF_None` and zero, then calls `ftCo_800C4E94`. The PR also added the new object to `obj_files.mk`, routed includes through the new header from hammer/pass users, removed stale declarations from `ft_0C31.h`, and typed `ftCo_800C6150` as taking `ftCo_GObj*`.
what_changed: Deleted the assembly bodies for `ftCo_800C60C8` and `ftCo_800C6110` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s`.

Removed the local `sdata2` constant label `ftCo_804D8DB8` from the assembly file; the matched C uses a literal zero argument for the motion-change call rather than preserving a separate assembly constant.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0C60.h` declaring the two matched functions with `ftCo_GObj*` arguments.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0C60.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` before the remaining `ft_0C31.s.o` assembly object.
Added `#include "ftCo_0C60.h"` to `ftCo_HammerWait.c`, `ftCo_HammerWalk.c`, and `ftCo_Pass.c`, indicating those files call into the newly split-out functions.
Cleaned `src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h` by removing the moved declarations and changing `ftCo_800C6150` from `Fighter_GObj*` to `ftCo_GObj*`.
The generated report records both functions and the file as 100% matched: `ftCo_800C60C8` at 72 bytes and `ftCo_800C6110` at 64 bytes.
smart_moves: Split a small address-range slice into its own `ftCo_0C60.c`/`.h` pair instead of leaving declarations in the broad `ft_0C31.h`, making ownership and include dependencies clearer.
Used existing domain types and helpers, especially `ftCo_GObj*`, `Fighter*`, and `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`, instead of keeping placeholder `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` signatures.

Kept the C implementation minimal and structurally faithful to the assembly: the boolean wrapper directly mirrors the branch on `ftCo_80099F1C`, while the helper mirrors motion-state change followed by fighter post-processing.
Updated dependent ftCommon source files to include the new focused header, avoiding reliance on broad or stale declarations.
Placed the new C object immediately before the still-remaining `ft_0C31.s.o` in `obj_files.mk`, preserving link/order expectations while peeling off matched code.
decomp_lessons: When extracting a small matched block from a large assembly file, remove only the matched symbols from `.s`, add the C object before the residual assembly object, and leave the remaining assembly symbols in place.
A wrapper function that returns 1 after a successful conditional call and 0 otherwise can often be expressed naturally as a `bool` with early/conditional returns; here that matched `ftCo_800C60C8`.
Use motion-state enums and flag enums when the immediate values are known: the assembly `0x137` and `0` became `ftCo_MS_HammerFall` and `Ft_MF_None`/`0` in `ftCo_800C6110`.
Replace broad placeholder declarations with focused headers once a slice is decompiled, and update callers to include the new owner header directly.
Header cleanup may reveal adjacent type fixes; this PR corrected `ftCo_800C6150` to use `ftCo_GObj*` while touching `ft_0C31.h`.
For ftCommon functions operating on fighter game objects, prefer `ftCo_GObj*` in declarations and use `GET_FIGHTER` when the underlying `Fighter*` is needed.
naming_conventions: New file and header names follow the address-slice convention: `ftCo_0C60.c` and `ftCo_0C60.h` for functions beginning at `0x800C60C8`.
Function names retain the existing address-based convention: `ftCo_800C60C8` and `ftCo_800C6110`.
The header guard used in the new header is `GALE01_0C60C8`, matching the starting address/game-id style present in some generated or transitional headers.
Motion-state and flag constants use project enum-style names: `ftCo_MS_HammerFall` and `Ft_MF_None`.
The code uses the ftCommon object typedef `ftCo_GObj*` rather than the broader `Fighter_GObj*` or placeholder parameter macros.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the `cmpwi`/`beq` branch in `ftCo_800C60C8` into a direct `if (ftCo_80099F1C(gobj)) { ... return true; } return false;` structure.
Preserved the call sequence in `ftCo_800C6110`: load/get fighter from `gobj`, call `ftCo_8009A184(gobj, ftCo_MS_HammerFall, Ft_MF_None, 0)`, then call `ftCo_800C4E94(fp)`.
Matched the assembly float-zero load used for the fourth argument to `ftCo_8009A184` by passing literal `0`, allowing removal of the `ftCo_804D8DB8` `.sdata2` constant from the assembly slice.
Used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` to reproduce the assembly `lwz r31, 0x2c(r3)` access while keeping the C idiomatic.
Registered the new C object in `TEXT_O_FILES` before the leftover assembly object so extracted code replaces the removed assembly without disrupting subsequent symbols.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
The PR body/report shows a 100.00% match for the file and both functions, but provides no reviewer discussion about the approach.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue decompiling functions after `ftCo_800C6150` in `ft_0C31.s`.
Search for other uses of `ftCo_800C60C8` to understand all hammer/pass transition call sites.
Check whether later cleanup renamed `ftCo_0C60.h` guard `GALE01_0C60C8` to a more file-based guard.
Search for other PRs replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` in ftCommon headers with `ftCo_GObj*` signatures.
Compare other `ftCo_8009A184` matches to confirm the same zero-argument tactic for float or rate parameters.
